mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% over 3 months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below 104 for 2 consecutive months signals a weakening demand environment that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 104 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in housing starts above 1.1 million units over 3 months signals an increase in supply that reduces housing prices and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.1 million units are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real GDP below 16250 for 2 consecutive quarters signals a weakening demand environment that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 16250 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% over 3 months signals a tightening monetary cycle that increases discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in initial unemployment claims below 200k for 2 consecutive weeks signals a strengthening labor market that increases consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 200k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in real personal income below 4400 for 2 consecutive months signals a weakening demand environment that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS < 4400 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
